issue: Recent wrong answers were not guaranteed to be shown newest to oldest by event time.
root_cause: The data layer built the wrong-answer list from an ascending-sorted attempt array and used slice(-5), which depends on upstream ordering and does not preserve explicit newest-first semantics. The payload also lacked a stable display timestamp.
effective_fix: Normalize attempt timestamps from all supported fields, sort the full incorrect-event set descending by timestamp, then take the first five records. Preserve ts and answeredAt in the report payload and defensively sort again in the UI before rendering.
failed_approach: UI-only sorting without fixing the report-data builder leaves cloud payloads and any other consumers with inconsistent ordering semantics.
risk_note: Week-over-week comparison logic may still mix display name and telemetry identity. That is a separate issue and should not be silently bundled into recent-wrong fixes.
verification_rule: For any recent-event feature, verify source event timestamps are preserved, full event sets are sorted before truncation, and rendered order is checked against explicit timestamps.
next_time_instruction: If touching parent-report or answer-history views, document data source, event grain, sorting key, dedupe key, and display order before patching.

issue: Long-running quality work regresses when project rules and lessons only exist in chat context.
root_cause: Repository-level engineering memory was missing outside subsystem-specific folders, so new iterations had no guaranteed root-level read/write loop for lessons, change history, and latest status.
effective_fix: Add a root AGENTS.md plus root logs/change_history.jsonl, logs/lessons_learned.jsonl, and reports/latest_iteration_report.md so each iteration has a mandatory pre-read and writeback target.
failed_approach: Relying on model memory or one-off prompts creates repeated rediscovery and repeated bug patterns.
risk_note: These files only help if future iterations read them first and keep them current.
verification_rule: Before meaningful edits, confirm the agent read AGENTS.md, change_history, lessons_learned, and latest_iteration_report; after edits, confirm all three writeback files were updated.
next_time_instruction: When a fix uncovers a reusable rule, convert it into a JSONL lesson with a concrete verification rule and a direct next-time instruction.

issue: After a wrong answer, pressing Enter could acknowledge the wrong-answer gate instead of re-checking the student's corrected answer.
root_cause: The wrong-answer state reused Enter as an acknowledge shortcut whenever focus was no longer in the answer input. After a submit interaction, focus could remain on the submit button, so the next Enter took the acknowledge branch instead of calling check().
effective_fix: After every wrong attempt, return focus to the answer input and treat Enter on the submit button the same as Enter in the answer field, so corrected answers are re-evaluated before any acknowledge shortcut runs.
failed_approach: Assuming the answer input always keeps focus after submit is unsafe in browser UI flows because buttons can retain focus and change keyboard behavior.
risk_note: This fixes the main Enter-path regression but does not change the product decision that moving to the next question after a wrong attempt still requires acknowledge or skip.
verification_rule: For any retryable form flow, test Enter with focus on the input and on the submit button after an error state. Confirm both paths re-run validation instead of triggering unrelated shortcuts.
next_time_instruction: When a wrong-answer gate and a submit action share Enter, verify focus transitions explicitly and prefer submit semantics over navigation semantics.

issue: Parent-report page had ~500 lines of inline domain logic duplicated from concept engines, making testing and maintenance difficult.
root_cause: All domain logic (weakness ranking, recommendation generation, copy text building, practice generation, report enrichment) was inline inside the HTML page IIFE, unreachable by automated tests.
effective_fix: Extract each domain into a standalone IIFE module on window.*, add script tags to load them before the page IIFE, replace inline functions with thin delegates that call through to shared engines with fallbacks, and add enrichReportData at both showDashboard and refreshReport entry points to pre-compute derived fields.
failed_approach: Attempting to replace 300+ lines inline with replace_string_in_file in one shot causes partial matches and orphaned code. Better to use PowerShell array slicing for large block removals.
risk_note: When using enrichReportData, weeklyFocus and recommendations are placed on report.d (not report root) because the page uses r = d.d as the main report object. Tests must check result.d.weeklyFocus, not result.weeklyFocus.
verification_rule: After extracting inline logic to shared engines, always: (1) add fallback in delegate functions, (2) verify enrichReportData is called in ALL data entry paths (not just the initial load), (3) run both spec tests and integration tests.
next_time_instruction: For large inline code extraction: first add script tags, then replace individual functions one at a time with delegates, then use PowerShell for bulk removal of orphaned code blocks. Never attempt to replace 300+ lines in one replace_string_in_file call.

lesson: Weakness cards should have clickable practice links, not just text descriptions. Use getTopicLink() from AIMathRecommendationEngine to resolve topics to module URLs. When DRYing duplicated logic (like moduleMap in renderRemedial vs TOPIC_LINK_MAP in recommendation_engine), prefer delegating to the shared engine. Always add analytics tracking for new clickable actions.
anti_pattern: Rendering next-action as plain text instead of a clickable link makes it impossible for parents to act on recommendations directly. Duplicating moduleMap across inline code and shared engines leads to drift when new modules are added.
effective_pattern: Delegate topic-to-URL resolution to getTopicLink() with fallback to star-pack. Add data-* attributes for analytics tracking on every action link.

lesson: The WoW comparison logic (prev week vs current week) depends on AIMathAttemptTelemetry which uses d.name as userId for localStorage lookup. When extracting to shared module, keep getPrevWeekAttempts as a separate function that wraps the telemetry call, making identity mapping testable. Always provide fallback inline logic in case the shared module fails to load. The WoW module only needs 3 exports: computeWoW (pure data transform), formatDelta (HTML formatting), getPrevWeekAttempts (telemetry wrapper).
anti_pattern: Computing prev-week stats inline inside renderDashboard makes it impossible to unit-test the comparison logic without a DOM. Also, the original inline code computed prevAvgMs but never displayed it ??dead code eliminated during extraction.
effective_pattern: Split WoW into pure computation (computeWoW) and telemetry access (getPrevWeekAttempts). The page wires them together. Tests can test computeWoW without mocking localStorage.

lesson: After extracting 10 shared engines from parent-report (iterations 5-11), all remaining inline code is view-layer: DOM manipulation, event handlers, HTML template rendering. Domain logic extraction is complete. The delegate-with-fallback pattern works well: each extraction adds ~20 lines of fallback but makes the domain logic independently testable. The extraction sequence matters: extract pure data transforms first (weakness, recommendation, report_data_builder), then time-windowed aggregations (wow, progress_trend, practice_summary), then output formatters (parent_copy, parent_advice). Test count grew from 10 ??42 across 11 iterations with zero regressions.
anti_pattern: Extracting interactive UI logic (practice card quiz flow, SVG rendering) into shared modules would create modules that still need DOM mocking, defeating the purpose. Stop extracting when only view-layer remains.
effective_pattern: For each extraction: (1) read inline code, (2) create pure-function IIFE module, (3) add script tag, (4) refactor inline to delegate with fallback, (5) create tests, (6) sync mirror, (7) validate + commit. One module per iteration keeps changes atomic and reviewable.

lesson: Question pages store attempt telemetry under a device UUID (u_abc123...) via AIMathCoachLog.getOrCreateUserId(), but parent-report was passing d.name (display name like '小�?') to listAttempts/getPrevWeekAttempts. This caused WoW comparison to always show 'no prior data'. The fix is to resolve the device UUID via getOrCreateUserId() for any localStorage telemetry query, and use d.name only for display and cloud API calls (recordPracticeResult).
anti_pattern: Using a human-readable display name as a storage key when a different system uses a UUID as the key. When two identity systems exist (cloud display name vs device UUID), always verify which key each API expects.
effective_pattern: Add a getDeviceUid() helper that resolves the correct localStorage identity, and use it consistently for all local telemetry queries. Keep d.name for cloud/display contexts only. Add a regression test that proves UUID resolves to stored data while display name does not.

lesson: The esc() helper in parent-report only escaped &, <, > but not quotes. When esc() output is used inside HTML attributes (data-weak-topic, href, etc.), unescaped quotes can break attribute boundaries and enable XSS. Standard HTML escaping must include double-quote (") and single-quote (') to be safe in attribute contexts. Current Chinese topic names don't contain quotes, but this is a latent vulnerability that could be exploited if data ever contains user-controlled strings.
anti_pattern: Escaping only angle brackets and ampersands while placing escaped output inside HTML attributes. The 3-char escape (&, <, >) is only safe for text content nodes, NOT for attribute contexts.
effective_pattern: Always escape all 5 HTML special characters: & < > " '. Add a regression test that verifies the esc() function includes quote escaping by reading the source code. When auditing escaping, check every call site to determine if output goes into text content or attribute values.

lesson: The inline practice answer checker used strict string comparison (normAns), so equivalent fractions like 2/4 were rejected when the stored answer was 1/2. Students who understand the concept get penalized for not simplifying. Fix: add parseFrac() + fractionsEqual() using cross-multiplication. Accept equivalent fractions but show a reminder about simplest form.
anti_pattern: Using string equality for math answers. Any numeric or fractional answer should be compared by value, not by string representation.
effective_pattern: Parse fraction strings (n/d) into {n, d} objects. Use cross-multiplication (a.n * b.d === b.n * a.d) for equivalence. Accept correct-but-unsimplified answers with a gentle simplification reminder. Add test cases for both equivalent and non-equivalent fractions.

lesson: Audited 8 escape/sanitize functions across 8 HTML pages. Found 3 patterns: (1) Full escaping with regex /[&<>"']/g ??safe in all contexts. (2) Partial escaping (& < > only) ??safe for text nodes, vulnerable in attributes. (3) No escaping (String() only) ??safe only for textContent, never innerHTML. The critical finding was exam-sprint escapeHtml() at line 815 which was type (2) but used in attribute context at line 1593 (data-qid). Pages with safe implementations: fraction-word-g5, commercial-pack1-fraction-sprint, interactive-g5-midterm1, interactive-g5-national-bank, report/index.html. No-escape safeStr() functions in exam-sprint, g5-grand-slam, and linear are currently safe because they're used only for textContent.
anti_pattern: Assuming all escape functions across pages are equivalent. Each page may have its own implementation with different coverage. Always audit all call sites when fixing an escape function.
effective_pattern: When auditing escape functions: (1) grep for all escape/sanitize function definitions, (2) check which HTML special chars each escapes, (3) check whether output is used in text or attribute context, (4) fix only those used in attribute context without full escaping. Add source-level regression tests that verify the function implementation includes required escaping.

lesson: Practice in parent-report had two modes: single (?�練一�? and quiz-3 (三�?小測�?. Only quiz-3 called persistPractice. Single-mode silently dropped results. This broke the commercial flow: student practices ??results should appear in summary. Fix: in goNext() for non-quiz mode, reset quizRecorded and call persistPractice per answered question. Also, practice results should write to local AIMathAttemptTelemetry (appendAttempt) so aggregate analytics can see them ??not just cloud recordPracticeResult.
anti_pattern: Only persisting results at quiz-end when single-practice mode also generates useful data. Assuming cloud writeback alone is sufficient when local analytics pipelines depend on local telemetry.
effective_pattern: Every practice interaction that yields a score/total should persist via both local telemetry (for analytics) and cloud (for report). Use source='parent-report-practice' to distinguish practice attempts from real quiz attempts. Reset quizRecorded flag per question in single mode to allow per-question persistence.

lesson: When explainWrongDetail and buildPracticeFromWrong only cover a few flagship kinds, the parent-report remediation loop degrades into generic advice for the rest of the existing bank. That weakens the commercial promise even if the links and writeback are correct. Existing bank families should have explicit remediation branches before introducing new content.
anti_pattern: Improving parent-report UI and writeback while leaving most wrong-answer kinds on a generic fallback. Parents see targeted cards, but the downstream explanation and similar-practice step still feels generic.
effective_pattern: Audit real bank kind families, add deterministic explanation/practice branches for uncovered existing kinds, and add regression tests that assert each family no longer falls through to the generic cause text. Keep generated answers integer-safe when possible and avoid hint text that trivially reveals the answer.

lesson: Handwritten kind lists are not enough for remediation coverage once multiple bank formats coexist. In this repo, bank payloads are exposed as window.*_BANK arrays in docs/<module>/bank.js rather than a shared const QUESTIONS wrapper. The safest regression pattern is to execute the real bank.js file in a VM sandbox, read the exported array from window, dedupe by topic+kind, and assert each representative bank family resolves to a non-generic explanation and non-empty fallback practice.
anti_pattern: Parsing bank.js with a hardcoded const QUESTIONS regex or testing only synthetic kind strings misses repo-specific wrappers and lets real module kinds drift away from remediation coverage.
effective_pattern: Load real bank.js payloads through vm.runInContext(), discover the exported bank array from window, and use bank-backed tests for commercial/life modules. Follow that with a future source-level audit so new bank kinds fail automatically when remediation mappings are missing.

lesson: A partial bank-backed audit is still too easy to outgrow. The durable gate is to scan every current docs/*/bank.js module, dedupe by topic+kind, and assert both explainWrongDetail and buildPracticeFromWrong stay non-generic across the whole repo. Some legacy bank files may not execute cleanly in vm because they end with a literal \n token after the assignment, so the loader needs a fallback that extracts the assigned JSON array directly.
anti_pattern: Assuming every bank.js file can be executed as-is in a VM, or limiting the audit to a hand-picked module list. That misses format outliers like interactive-g5-midterm1 and interactive-g5-national-bank and weakens the value of the gate.
effective_pattern: Use a two-step loader: try vm execution first, then fall back to parsing window.* = [...] assignments with a regex that tolerates trailing literal \n. Run this audit as part of the parent-report test suite so new bank kinds break CI immediately instead of silently shipping generic remediation.

lesson: The first screen is more commercially useful when it surfaces the top weak concepts directly beside the KPIs and next actions, instead of forcing the parent to open a deeper section to understand why the system is recommending action. The safest implementation is not a new ranking path; it is a compact view over the existing ranked weakness list using the same reason/action delegates and stable topic links.
anti_pattern: Adding a new first-screen weakness panel with its own ranking or copy rules. That creates drift between the quick summary and the deeper weakness/remediation sections and weakens trust when the messages diverge.
effective_pattern: Render at most 3 weakness summary cards from the already-ranked weakness array, reuse describeWeakReason() and nextAction(), and include a direct practice CTA. Guard it with a source-level regression test that checks the cap, the explanation text, and the practice link CTA.

lesson: Parents trust a first-screen recommendation faster when each weak-area card shows a tiny piece of concrete evidence, not just the label and suggested action. A short sentence using existing weakness signals like wrong-count and high-hint-count is enough to make the recommendation feel grounded without adding another chart or section.
anti_pattern: Making the first screen more persuasive by adding separate scoring logic or heavy analytics markup. That increases drift and cognitive load instead of making the recommendation more understandable.
effective_pattern: Keep the first-screen weakness card compact: topic, why weak, evidence sentence, what to do now, direct practice link. Reuse the existing weakness object fields and enforce the evidence line in the summary regression test so future refactors do not silently remove it.

lesson: Once the first-screen weakness evidence sentence exists, leaving it assembled inline in the page recreates the same drift problem the shared engines were meant to solve. The evidence sentence should live in the weakness engine beside reason and next-action text, so any future copy or scoring-field change has one source of truth.
anti_pattern: Keeping evidence text as page-local string assembly while reason and next action already come from the shared weakness engine. That creates a second formatting rule path and makes the quick summary more likely to diverge from the rest of the report.
effective_pattern: Expose a small `buildWeaknessEvidenceText()` helper from `AIMathWeaknessEngine`, reuse it from the parent-report page, and add a regression test that checks both the helper output and the page?�s delegation to it.

lesson: Once the shared weakness engine owns the evidence sentence, deeper weakness tables and remedial cards should render that same sentence rather than reassembling wrong-count and hint-count copy inline. Reusing the same formatter keeps first-screen and deeper trust signals aligned when copy or weakness fields change.
anti_pattern: Letting deeper parent-report sections keep their own evidence string templates after the summary card has already moved to a shared formatter. That recreates drift across the same report page and makes copy changes easy to miss.
effective_pattern: Store shared evidence text on recommendation objects, render it directly in detailed remedial cards, and add a source-level regression test that fails if the old inline evidence template reappears.

lesson: If the browser must still write parent-report data directly, the least-bad interim pattern is a session-scoped runtime token with explicit setter/clear helpers, not a bundled global config token or a long-lived localStorage PAT. That reduces accidental exposure and persistence while preserving the current write flow until a backend-owned path exists.
anti_pattern: Reading a write token from `window.AIMathCloudSyncConfig.gistToken` or storing it persistently in localStorage. Both patterns make a repo-clean build still commercially unsafe because the secret path is browser-visible and sticky across sessions.
effective_pattern: Read the write token from sessionStorage, migrate and delete the legacy localStorage key once, expose `setCloudWriteToken()` / `clearCloudWriteToken()` for explicit runtime use, and add a regression test that fails if bundle/global token injection or persistent lookup comes back.

lesson: The smallest reversible step away from browser-owned parent-report writes is a dedicated backend registry keyed by normalized name plus server-validated PIN, not a broad auth-system rewrite. Keep the current name+PIN UX, move PIN verification and storage to the backend, and let the static frontend only send report_data/practice_event payloads to POST endpoints.
anti_pattern: Patching the browser write path in place with more client-side token rules, or preserving browser-side `entry.pin` comparison after adding a backend fetch endpoint. That keeps authority in the wrong tier and duplicates verification logic.
effective_pattern: Add SQLite-backed fetch/upsert endpoints with hashed PIN storage, pass the PIN from unlock/refresh/practice flows into the backend, return report data without the PIN, and gate the static frontend with a configurable backend base (`AIMATH_PARENT_REPORT_API_BASE`, `AIMATH_API_BASE`, or `?api=`). Add one backend endpoint test and one source-level frontend regression test so the main write path cannot silently fall back to direct Gist PATCH again.

lesson: When adding subscription-gated endpoints alongside existing free endpoints, build the adapter with automatic fallback: paid path (X-API-Key + student_id) tries the subscription-gated endpoint first, and on 402/401/404 automatically falls through to the free path (name+PIN). This prevents paid-path failures from being user-visible while the credential provisioning UI is still being built. Store paid credentials in sessionStorage (not localStorage) because they are session-scoped secrets that should not persist across browser restarts.
anti_pattern: Requiring the entire frontend to switch to the paid path at once, or storing API keys in localStorage where they persist indefinitely. Also: adding new endpoint auth patterns without writing deny-by-default tests that verify the gate is actually checked.
effective_pattern: Add setCredentials/clearCredentials/hasCredentials to the adapter, gate the paid path on both hasCredentials AND isPaid(), fall through to free path on any paid-path error, and add a source-level regression test that fails if credentials use localStorage or if the paid path is accessible without subscription check.

lesson: Iterative security hardening: first pass (iter 39) establishes architecture (adapter, subscription endpoints, credential flow), but typically leaves legacy code paths that still leak secrets or sensitive data. A dedicated second pass (iter 40) is needed to audit every remaining usage of the old infrastructure. Pattern: 1) search for all remaining usages of old auth mechanisms via source-level grep, 2) for each usage determine if it's read-only or write, 3) remove auth headers from read-only paths (public Gist reads don't need tokens), 4) strip sensitive fields (PINs) from uncontrolled data sources, 5) write source-level regression tests that grep the actual code to prevent reintroduction. Source-level tests are more durable than mock-based tests for security invariants because they catch problems at commit time regardless of runtime conditions.

When extending a dual-path adapter to cover a new operation (practice events), follow the established pattern exactly: check _isPaidAndCredentialed(), route to subscription-gated endpoint with X-API-Key, fall through to free path on error. The corresponding backend endpoint must reuse the same 3-gate pattern (get_account_by_api_key + ensure_subscription_active + _verify_student_ownership) and the same sanitizer for the data type. Removing dead exports is safe once confirmed by source-level grep: search all HTML and JS files for the symbol name, verify 0 callers outside the definition file, then remove from export block while retaining internal functions for gradual cleanup. Add source-level regression test that the export block does NOT contain the removed symbols.

When wiring a paid bootstrap path that enables backend writeback, the subscription state confirmed by the backend should be SESSION-SCOPED (in-memory variable), not persisted to localStorage. This prevents a user who opens the page without credentials from retaining paid status from a previous session. The pattern: URL params ??sessionStorage credentials ??async whoami validation ??in-memory _backendPaidStatus ??getEffectiveSub() override. Three independent gates protect the paid path: (1) credentials in sessionStorage, (2) isPaid() returns true, (3) backend rejects invalid keys on every API call.
anti_pattern: Using activatePaidPlan() or confirmPayment() to sync backend-confirmed subscription state ??these persist to localStorage and survive across sessions, creating a privilege escalation vector where a user retains paid access after their subscription expires.
effective_pattern: Use an in-memory variable (_backendPaidStatus) set by syncFromBackend() and cleared on page unload. Hook into getEffectiveSub() between the hardcoded override (UNLIMITED_STUDENT_NAMES) and the localStorage fallback. Strip credentials from URL via history.replaceState immediately after reading them ??prevents sharing, bookmarking, or browser history exposure of API keys.

lesson: When wiring a paid bootstrap path that enables backend writeback, the subscription state confirmed by the backend should be SESSION-SCOPED (in-memory variable), not persisted to localStorage. This prevents a user who opens the page without credentials from retaining paid status from a previous session. The pattern: URL params to sessionStorage credentials to async whoami validation to in-memory _backendPaidStatus to getEffectiveSub() override. Three independent gates protect the paid path: (1) credentials in sessionStorage, (2) isPaid() returns true, (3) backend rejects invalid keys on every API call.
anti_pattern: Using activatePaidPlan() or confirmPayment() to sync backend-confirmed subscription state - these persist to localStorage and survive across sessions, creating a privilege escalation vector where a user retains paid access after their subscription expires.
effective_pattern: Use an in-memory variable (_backendPaidStatus) set by syncFromBackend() and cleared on page unload. Hook into getEffectiveSub() between the hardcoded override (UNLIMITED_STUDENT_NAMES) and the localStorage fallback. Strip credentials from URL via history.replaceState immediately after reading them - prevents sharing, bookmarking, or browser history exposure of API keys.

lesson: When passing credentials from a server-side APP to a browser-facing page, never put long-lived API keys in URL params. Instead use a short-lived, single-use bootstrap token: APP calls POST /bootstrap server-side with real API key to get an opaque token (secrets.token_urlsafe(32), 5-min TTL, stored in-memory dict). APP puts ?bt=<token> in URL. Frontend exchanges token via POST body to get real credentials. Token is popped (consumed) on first use. The exchange endpoint must re-validate subscription to prevent stale tokens from granting access after subscription expiry.
anti_pattern: Passing raw api_key in URL params (?api_key=xxx) exposes it in browser history, server access logs, referrer headers, analytics beacons, and shareable deep links. Even with history.replaceState() stripping, the key is visible during navigation and in the Address bar briefly.
effective_pattern: Bootstrap token exchange: (1) server-side POST with real X-API-Key -> opaque token, (2) URL carries only short-lived token, (3) frontend POST exchanges token for credentials via response body, (4) token is single-use (dict.pop), (5) frontend actively rejects raw api_key in URL with user warning. Source-level test window size must account for the full endpoint body (use 1000+ chars, not 800).

lesson: When adding rate limiting to bootstrap/exchange endpoints: (1) Use per-IP sliding window with separate limits per endpoint type (bootstrap stricter since it creates server state, exchange more generous since tokens are already single-use). (2) Add per-account outstanding token cap to prevent token flooding separate from IP rate limits. (3) Check rate limit BEFORE auth gates so even invalid requests count against the limit. (4) Use HTTP 429 with descriptive detail for limit hits. (5) Source-level tests need larger window sizes when endpoint body grows ??use 1500+ chars for endpoints with rate limiter + token cap logic. (6) Tests should temporarily lower limits and clear rate limit state to avoid flaky tests.
anti_pattern: Checking rate limit AFTER auth validation lets unauthenticated attackers probe without rate limit consequences. Adding rate limiting without token caps lets authenticated users flood the token store.
effective_pattern: Rate limit check runs first (before auth gates), per-account cap runs after auth (needs account_id), both return 429. Tests use fixture-scoped cleanup and temporarily lowered limits for deterministic behavior.

lesson: When moving from in-memory dict to DB-backed token store: (1) Store SHA-256 hash of token, never the raw token ??defense in depth against DB access. (2) Use _consume_bootstrap_token() with SELECT + UPDATE pattern (check unconsumed + unexpired, then set consumed_at). (3) Rate limiter moves to its own table (rate_limit_events) with key+ts columns and sliding window via DELETE + COUNT + INSERT. (4) Tests that previously manipulated in-memory dicts must switch to DB operations ??UPDATE for expiry manipulation, DELETE FROM for state clearing. (5) Source-level JS tests that check for '.pop(' must update to check for '_consume_bootstrap_token' since the consumption pattern changed. (6) Add a DB persistence test that verifies the token exists in DB and consumed_at is set after exchange. (7) Cleanup expired tokens with 2x TTL buffer to handle edge cases.
anti_pattern: Storing raw tokens in DB (allows token theft from DB access). Using in-memory dicts that are lost on server restart. Tests that reference module-level dict attributes (_bootstrap_tokens, _rate_limit_store) which break when the storage backend changes.
effective_pattern: Hash tokens before storing. Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS in init_db(). Tests manipulate DB rows directly via conn = setup_server.db(). Source-level tests check for function names (_consume_bootstrap_token) not implementation details (.pop).

lesson: When building a login UI that feeds into an existing multi-step token exchange flow: (1) Raw api_key from login response must stay in closure scope only - declare as local var in the innermost practical scope, use for bootstrap X-API-Key header, then let it go out of scope. (2) readReportSnapshot() returns {data: report_payload, cloud_ts, source} - must pass entry.data (not entry) to showDashboard() since showDashboard checks d.d. (3) Clear password from DOM immediately after successful exchange. (4) Source-level security tests that count adapter.setCredentials calls must use regex with trailing ( to match actual calls, not property access checks like !adapter.setCredentials. (5) Collapsible <details> element with CSS arrow rotation on toggle is a low-risk, accessible UI pattern for optional login sections.
anti_pattern: Passing raw readReportSnapshot() entry to showDashboard() without extracting .data first. Counting method references (adapter.setCredentials) instead of method calls (adapter.setCredentials() with paren) in source-level tests. Storing loginApiKey in any persistent storage even temporarily.
effective_pattern: 3-step flow (login->bootstrap->exchange) keeps raw api_key ephemeral. Error responses (401/402/403) handled individually with user-facing Chinese messages. Button disabled during async flow, re-enabled on ALL error paths (counted 5+ re-enable points). Auto-load report on success via readReportSnapshot reduces friction.

lesson: When adding rate limiting to an auth endpoint: (1) Rate limit check MUST fire BEFORE credential validation to avoid information leakage (attacker cannot distinguish valid-username-wrong-password from rate-limited). (2) The 429 response body must contain only a generic message, never credentials or username. (3) Login deserves a stricter limit than bootstrap (5/min vs 10/min) because login is the primary brute-force target. (4) Reusing the existing _check_rate_limit(key, max) pattern with a new key prefix (login:{ip}) is the minimal safe change. (5) Test must verify that a wrong-password attempt AFTER rate limit is exceeded returns 429 (not 401, which would leak that the IP was rate-limited but credentials were checked).
anti_pattern: Checking credentials before rate limit (leaks valid usernames via timing or response code differences). Returning detailed error info in 429 response. Using the same rate limit threshold for login as for authenticated endpoints.
effective_pattern: Rate limit as first operation in endpoint handler. Generic 429 detail message. Stricter limit for unauthenticated credential-guessing endpoints. Test both the rate limit itself AND the ordering guarantee (rate limit before credential check).

lesson: When adding rate limiting to a login endpoint: (1) Rate limit check MUST fire before ANY credential validation (DB lookup, password check) to prevent timing attacks that reveal whether a username exists. (2) Login rate limit should be stricter than bootstrap/exchange (5/min vs 10/20) since login involves password guessing. (3) The 429 response message must be generic - never include username, password, or api_key in the response body. (4) Reusing existing _check_rate_limit(key, max) infrastructure is zero-risk - just add a new constant and a 3-line rate limit block at the top of the endpoint. (5) Test the ordering property explicitly: exhaust rate limit with bad credentials (getting 401s), then verify next attempt returns 429 (not 401), proving the rate limiter fires first. (6) JS source-level test can verify ordering by checking that _check_rate_limit appears before WHERE au.username in the source text.
anti_pattern: Checking rate limit AFTER credential validation (leaks timing info about valid usernames). Using the same rate limit as bootstrap (10/min is too generous for password guessing). Including error details in 429 response.
effective_pattern: Rate limit fires first (before any DB query). Stricter limit for login (5/min). Generic 429 message. Three-test coverage pattern: (a) happy path rate limit, (b) ordering proof, (c) no-leak proof.

lesson: Account-level lockout complements IP-based rate limiting by preventing credential stuffing from distributed IPs. Key design: (1) lockout check fires AFTER IP rate limit but BEFORE credential validation �X this prevents the lockout itself from leaking whether a username exists. (2) Record failures for both invalid usernames and wrong passwords to avoid username enumeration. (3) Clear ALL failure records on successful login to prevent stale lockouts. (4) Use a separate table (login_failures) from rate_limit_events to keep concerns isolated. (5) HTTP 423 (Locked) is semantically distinct from 429 (Too Many Requests), allowing clients to differentiate IP-based vs account-based throttling.

When adding admin endpoints to a FastAPI app that has a catch-all app.mount("/", StaticFiles(...)) at the bottom, the admin endpoint MUST be defined BEFORE the mount. Otherwise FastAPI routes the request to the static file handler first and returns 404. Also: when testing structured logging via extra={} kwargs, the test handler must capture the LogRecord object (not just the formatted string) to verify extra fields like username, reason, client_ip. Use handler.emit = lambda record: captured.append(record) pattern.

When refactoring a login flow to add a student selector for multi-student accounts: (1) Return all students in the login response (not just LIMIT 1) while preserving default_student for backward compat. (2) Extract bootstrap+exchange into a named helper (proceedWithStudent) to avoid deep nesting ??the helper can be called from single-student auto-path, multi-student selector, or fallback. (3) Use cloneNode+replaceChild to avoid stacking event listeners on dynamically shown selector buttons. (4) Source-level security tests that count api_key usages need threshold updates when code is refactored into helper functions with parameter passing ??augment with a stricter storage-API negative check instead.

When removing a legacy external dependency (Gist read fallback), the clean removal is only possible because the codebase was already well-architected: (1) the adapter pattern shielded the parent-report page from direct Gist knowledge, (2) all write paths had already been migrated to the backend in prior iterations, (3) security tests had already verified Gist safety properties. The removal itself is simple: delete constants, delete fallback block, delete dead helpers. But the prerequisites (backend registry, adapter abstraction, test coverage) took 10+ iterations to establish. Also: search for ALL references (including comments/JSDoc) to the removed infrastructure �X stale comments like registry.json and gist token in JSDoc/console.warn will fail strict source-level tests.

When extending an admin endpoint with summary statistics, the response body grows significantly. Source-level JS tests that slice a fixed-char window from the endpoint's starting position must use a window size large enough to capture the full return statement. Measure with indexOf + length in the real source before setting the window constant. For this endpoint the summary dict pushed the return statement to offset 1891 from the route decorator, requiring a 2200-char window.

When designing auth recovery flows for an MVP without email infrastructure: (1) Admin-assisted reset is the simplest viable path �X reuses existing admin-token auth, requires only 1 new endpoint, no external services. (2) PIN-based reset sounds convenient but conflates two credential domains and PINs are brute-forceable. (3) Email-based reset is the commercial-grade solution but requires schema migration + external service �X plan this as a follow-up. (4) Always note the password hashing algorithm �X SHA-256 is adequate for salted passwords but bcrypt/argon2 is the modern standard and should be upgraded when touching password infrastructure.
anti_pattern: Do not conflate parent-report PIN auth (per-student, 4-6 digit) with account login credentials (per-account, full password). They serve different trust boundaries and should not be cross-linked for password reset.
effective_strategy: Design-only iterations for high-risk auth changes: analyze architecture, evaluate multiple options with explicit pros/cons/risks, document impacted files and validation plan, then stop for human review. This prevents premature implementation of security-sensitive features.

lesson: When audit tools use regex windows (src.match(...)[0] with a 2000-char capture), JSDoc comments and function definitions can match as false positives. The fix: match actual CALLS by their assignment context (html += buildXYZ()) rather than any bare function-name reference. Always verify audit detection with a regression injection test after changing detection logic.
anti_pattern: Do not match bare function names in audit checks when the source includes JSDoc comments and function definitions near the search anchor. The function definition itself and its doc comment will produce false matches.
effective_strategy: Use assignment-context matching (+=) to distinguish actual rendering calls from definitions, exports, and documentation. Verify with injection test: temporarily inject a real regression, confirm audit catches it, then restore.

issue: No deterministic health check endpoint existed for operational monitoring tools.
root_cause: Existing /health endpoint returned a timestamp, making response non-deterministic. Standard /healthz convention was missing.
effective_fix: Added GET /healthz returning {"status":"ok"} with no dynamic fields. Kept existing /health for backward compatibility.
verification_rule: GET /healthz must return exactly {"status":"ok"} with 200 status.
next_time_instruction: When adding infrastructure endpoints, prefer Kubernetes-convention paths (/healthz, /readyz, /livez) and ensure responses are fully deterministic with no secrets.

issue: The product-critical cross-device flow needed proof that a student on Device A and a parent on Device B can see the same report data.
root_cause: The implementation already existed across several files, but there was no comprehensive regression suite proving the full name+PIN cloud round-trip and no fresh iteration writeback documenting the exact chain and its dependency on backend-base configuration.
effective_fix: Audit the full browser-to-backend path first, then add end-to-end API tests that cover full round-trip, incremental sync, name normalization, credential rejection, event accumulation, freshness timestamps, tenant isolation, unicode names, and larger payload sizes. Confirm docs/dist mirrors stay in sync while leaving production logic unchanged.
failed_approach: Assuming the flow was incomplete and changing working runtime code before proving the existing chain. For integration-heavy flows, verification-first is lower risk than speculative rewrites.
risk_note: Cloud sync still depends on a configured backend base via AIMATH_PARENT_REPORT_API_BASE, AIMATH_API_BASE, or ?api=. If that is never set on the student device, no remote sync occurs.
verification_rule: For any cross-device report flow, verify: student write path triggers backend upsert, parent fetch path validates credentials server-side, wrong PIN returns 403, unknown student returns 404, cloud_ts updates, and separate students cannot cross-read data.
next_time_instruction: Before changing parent-report sync semantics, trace the full identity and transport chain end to end and add round-trip tests first; do not rewrite working sync code based on assumptions.

issue: No password recovery mechanism existed for paid parent accounts. Locked-out or forgotten-password users had no recovery path.
root_cause: Auth system had no reset endpoint. Only provision created credentials.
effective_fix: Added POST /v1/app/admin/reset-password: admin-token gated, generates temp password, updates hash/salt, clears login_failures.
failed_approach: Initial lockout test used repeated HTTP login calls from same IP, which triggered IP-level rate limit (429) alongside account lockout (423). Fixed by inserting failure records directly into DB.
risk_note: Temp password must be communicated out-of-band (admin to parent). SHA-256 hashing still in use.
verification_rule: 4 tests: no-token ??401, unknown-user ??404, happy-path ??200 + old pw fails + temp pw works, clears-failures ??lockout cleared.
next_time_instruction: When testing account lockout flows, avoid triggering IP-level rate limits by inserting login_failures records directly into the DB instead of making repeated HTTP calls from the same test client IP.

lesson: Pricing page (docs/pricing/index.html) was saved in Big5 encoding since its creation commit (fcf7f8c46), despite declaring charset=UTF-8. Automated question-count update scripts that read as UTF-8 and wrote back created 2444 U+FFFD replacement characters (commits d061a9316 onward). Root fix: convert file from Big5 to actual UTF-8 via [System.Text.Encoding]::GetEncoding('big5') read + UTF-8 write. Prevention: future count-update scripts must verify UTF-8 round-trip integrity or use byte-level replacement.

UI-only display:none hiding of dev/mock controls is insufficient for production hardening. Functions remain callable from browser console. Minimum viable hardening: add a runtime guard variable (window.__AIMATH_DEV__) that must be explicitly set before mock functions execute. This is stronger than query-param gating alone because (a) it requires console access, (b) it does not persist across page loads, and (c) it cannot be linked to. However, underlying subscription.js methods remain accessible since they are shared with production checkout flow ??true server-side payment state is needed for complete security.

anti_patterns: Never trust localStorage alone for subscription state ??always verify against server when credentials are available
Never gate the verify endpoint behind ensure_subscription_active ??it needs to return status for ALL states including inactive
lesson: Server-side subscription verification requires: 1) Webhook endpoint with HMAC signature verification to receive Stripe events. 2) A non-402 verify endpoint that returns status for frontend reconciliation. 3) Frontend verifyWithServer() that compares server truth against localStorage and overrides if mismatched. The hmac module provides lightweight Stripe signature verification without requiring the stripe Python package.
effective_patterns: HMAC-SHA256 webhook signature verification using Python hmac module
Separate verify endpoint that returns status without 402 gate (unlike whoami)
Frontend reconciliation: server-says-inactive + local-says-paid = demote to free
sessionStorage for api_key persistence across page navigation within session

lesson: Parent report loading feedback is critical for trust. Plain text status messages (�?��?�詢) appear frozen on slow connections. An animated spinner immediately signals progress. For locked feature overlays, showing 1-2 concrete data insights (weak concept names, weekly rate delta) outperforms generic ?��??�可�?? text for conversion because parents see personalized value before deciding to pay.
effective_patterns: CSS @keyframes spinner with border-top-color trick for lightweight loading indicator
cls=loading parameter on showStatus/setPaidMsg for consistent spinner insertion
Preview hints extracted from already-computed chart data (no extra computation)
3-step troubleshooting checklist in error messages reduces support burden
anti_patterns: Never show only text status during multi-second async operations ??always show animated feedback
Never use generic 密碼?�誤 ??always tell user what kind of password (4-6 digit parent PIN)

lesson: Wiring concept_taxonomy resolve_concept_ids into recordAttempt is a zero-risk, high-value foundation step. The TOPIC_TAG_TO_CONCEPT mapping covers the main tag families (fraction, decimal, volume, percent, ratio, etc.) but unknown tags silently produce empty concept_ids. This is acceptable because the column default is already []. The key insight: skill_tags from existing question packs map cleanly to concept_ids without any event schema changes  the topic field adds additional coverage. concept_points in extra dict provides a third resolution path for questions that include structured concept metadata.
anti_pattern: Do not attempt to wire all Phase 1-5 modules at once. Each should be wired in a separate iteration with its own tests and regression check.
effective_pattern: Wire one module per iteration: import, add 5-15 lines in recordAttempt, UPDATE the corresponding column, return the new data in the response, add 5-10 focused integration tests.

lesson: classify_error uses keyword-only arguments: is_correct, user_answer, correct_answer, response_time_sec, used_hints, hint_levels_shown, changed_answer, meta. The correct_answer must be extracted from v.extra.get(correct_answer) since it is not a top-level field. Duration must be converted from ms to seconds. For correct answers, skip classification entirely (error_type=None). ErrorType enum values are strings via .value property.
anti_pattern: Do not call classify_error on correct answers - it returns None but wastes CPU. Guard with if not v.is_correct.
effective_pattern: Pattern: guard on is_correct, extract supporting fields from extra dict, call with keyword args, UPDATE column only when result is not None.

lesson: Wiring mastery_engine is the critical foundational step that unlocks EXP-04 through EXP-09. update_mastery is a pure function: takes (StudentConceptState, AnswerEvent) and returns (updated_state, MasteryActions). get_concept_state auto-creates UNBUILT default. upsert_concept_state uses ON CONFLICT UPDATE for idempotent writes. The AnswerEvent dataclass wraps 10 fields from the attempt event. error_type from EXP-03 is passed as a field. 4 modules now wired: concept_taxonomy, error_classifier, mastery_engine, concept_state.
anti_pattern: Do not batch all concept updates in a single SQL statement. Each concept needs its own get-compute-upsert cycle because state depends on prior state.
effective_pattern: For-each pattern: iterate concept_ids, get state per concept, compute update, upsert individually. Keep it inside the same transaction (conn not committed until after all updates).

lesson: Read-only endpoints are the safest integration wiring. Pattern: import get_all_states in try/except block with None fallback, add GET endpoint with X-API-Key auth, verify student belongs to account, open learning DB connection, call get_all_states, serialize to dict. Zero write side effects. The endpoint follows existing patterns (learning_connect + ensure_learning_schema in try/finally block).
anti_pattern: Do not expose raw dataclass objects directly. Always serialize to dict with selected fields to control the API surface.
effective_pattern: Serialize concept state to dict with only the fields frontends need: mastery_level, mastery_score, attempts_total, correct_total, recent_accuracy, hint_dependency, consecutive_correct, consecutive_wrong, needs_review, last_seen_at.

lesson: Best integration pattern: when the upstream module (mastery_engine) already computes the signal (remediation_needed, calm_mode_entered), the wiring layer just surfaces it in the response. No new logic needed. remediation_concepts is a derived list: [cid for m in mastery_updates if m.remediation_needed]. This pattern keeps service.py thin.
anti_pattern: Do not recompute remediation logic in service.py - the mastery engine already handles it via consecutive_wrong threshold.
effective_pattern: Surface existing signals: add fields to mastery entry dict, aggregate into a top-level convenience list.

lesson: Gamification functions (check_unlocks, compute_badges) are pure computations over List[StudentConceptState]. After mastery updates, call get_all_states(student_id, conn=conn) to get fresh states, then pass to both functions. No DB writes needed. Streak tracking (update_streak) requires schema extension for persisting streak data - deferred to future iteration.
anti_pattern: Do not try to compute streak without persistent storage. update_streak needs a stored Streak object to track consecutive days.
effective_pattern: Import get_all_states alongside get_concept_state. After mastery loop, get_all_states gives complete picture for gamification. Serialize UnlockStatus and Badge to flat dicts for JSON response.

lesson: Same endpoint pattern as R4 concept-state: import in try block, add GET with auth, query main DB for student IDs (class_students table), then call learning module with get_class_states. Key conversion: get_class_states returns Dict[str, Dict[str, State]] but generate_teacher_report expects Dict[str, List[State]], so convert with {sid: list(m.values())}.
anti_pattern: Do not try to pass Dict[str, Dict[str, State]] directly to generate_teacher_report - it expects List not Dict as inner type.
effective_pattern: Query class_students for IDs, get_class_states for mastery data, convert format, generate report, serialize with report_to_dict.

lesson: Same endpoint pattern as R7 teacher report but for parent audience. get_all_concept_states returns Dict[str, StudentConceptState], convert to list via .values() for generate_parent_concept_progress which expects List[StudentConceptState]. Chinese labels (MASTERY_LABELS_ZH) and MASTERY_PARENT_TIPS are built into the module.
anti_pattern: Do not pass dict directly - parent report function expects a list of states, not dict.
effective_pattern: Reuse auth+ownership pattern from concept-state endpoint. States dict values() gives list for parent report generator.

lesson: When inserting a new Pydantic model class between existing classes in code, verify that fields are not accidentally moved from one class to another. The PracticeNextRequest model lost topic_key and seed fields when ConceptNextRequest was added, causing a regression. Virtual QuestionItem pool from CONCEPT_TAXONOMY works well: 3 items per concept (easy/normal/hard) gives 123 items for 41 concepts. select_next_item returns strategy and Chinese-language reason text.
anti_pattern: Do not insert new class definitions in the middle of an existing class definition. Always verify neighboring class definitions are intact after edit.
effective_pattern: Build virtual QuestionItem pool from CONCEPT_TAXONOMY dict. Each concept gets 3 difficulty variants. is_application=True for hard items.

lesson: Hardcoded future timestamps in tests drift outside analytics windows as wall clock advances. Always use datetime.now()-timedelta(days=N) for timestamps that feed into time-windowed analytics (e.g. 30-day skill stats). This applies to any test that calls getStudentAnalytics or similar windowed queries.
anti_pattern: Using fixed timestamps like 2026-02-01 in tests that rely on 30-day windows. These tests silently break months after being written.
effective_pattern: Define _recent_iso(days_ago) helper that returns (datetime.now()-timedelta(days=days_ago)).isoformat(). Use it for all timestamps. Relax trend count assertions to >= rather than exact equality since window-relative data varies.

lesson: When tests set environment variables to enable external generators (EXTERNAL_WEB_FRACTION_DECIMAL, EXTERNAL_WEB_QUESTION_BANK), both engine AND server modules must be reloaded. Reloading only server.py without reloading engine.py first means GENERATORS dict does not pick up the external generator, causing topic_key lookups to fall through to default generators.
anti_pattern: importlib.reload(server) without first doing importlib.reload(engine). The server module imports from engine at module level, but engine reads env vars at module level too.
effective_pattern: import engine; importlib.reload(engine); importlib.reload(server). Order matters: engine first, then server. Also be aware of cross-contamination between tests that enable different env vars.

lesson: HTML template tests should assert on template variable names or structural elements, not on computed values that depend on mock data. Checking for literal values like 58 is fragile when the data source changes. Checking for risk_score (the template variable) is robust.
anti_pattern: assert "58" in html -- breaks when mock data changes. assert "teacher/class rollup" -- breaks due to case mismatch with actual HTML.
effective_pattern: assert "risk_score" in html -- checks template structure. For case-sensitive content, read the actual HTML source first and match exactly.

lesson: Existing hint data pipeline (hints_viewed_count, hint_steps_viewed_json, la_hint_usage, concept_ids_json) provides sufficient data for computing hint effectiveness without schema changes. A pure SQL+Python aggregation function over la_attempt_events WHERE hints_viewed_count > 0 gives hint_success_rate, stuck_after_hint_rate, by_level distribution, and by_concept breakdown. Key design choice: use hints_viewed_count as the level indicator (1=saw 1 hint, 2=saw 2 hints, etc.) rather than trying to correlate with HintLevel enum from remediation_flow.py.
anti_pattern: Do not add new columns or tables just to compute analytics that can be derived from existing data. Do not try to correlate remediation_flow in-memory HintLevel with stored hints_viewed_count until the link is verified.
effective_pattern: Read-only analytics functions over existing tables are zero-risk. Functions should accept optional student_id (None=class-wide) and window_days for flexibility. Return generated_at timestamp for cache validation.

lesson: httpx.ASGITransport with importlib.reload(server) is the standard pattern for endpoint integration tests in this codebase. Optional query params (student_id=None for class-wide) are more flexible than requiring a path parameter.
anti_pattern: Do not use path parameters like /v1/student/{student_id}/hint-effectiveness when the parameter is optional - use query params instead.
effective_pattern: GET endpoints with optional student_id query param enable both per-student and class-wide queries without needing separate endpoints.

lesson: Chinese-language summaries with risk thresholds (40% success, 50% stuck rate) provide actionable teacher guidance. Sorting by_concept by success_rate ascending puts weakest areas first. Minimum attempt threshold (>=3) prevents false risk flags.
anti_pattern: Do not flag concepts with few attempts as at-risk - insufficient data causes false alarms.
effective_pattern: Use configurable thresholds for risk classification and sort output to surface most critical items first.

lesson: Direct unit testing of update_mastery() reveals that EMA recent_accuracy moves slowly (alpha=0.3) but score deltas cause faster level changes. Gate-based promotion is robust. REVIEW_NEEDED at rank 2 prevents demotion by design. Stacking penalties can reach -0.33 per event.
anti_pattern: Do not test mastery edge cases only through recordAttempt integration - too many variables obscure boundary behavior.
effective_pattern: Create StudentConceptState with precise initial values to isolate specific edge case behavior in update_mastery().

lesson: Transfer (+0.12) and delayed_review (+0.10) bonuses stack with correct_no_hint (+0.15). Remediation and calm_mode share threshold (3 consecutive wrong), always trigger together. REVIEW_NEEDED can re-promote to MASTERED via same gate as APPROACHING_MASTERY.
anti_pattern: Do not assume remediation and calm_mode are independent - they share the same threshold in current config.
effective_pattern: Test both the trigger condition (>=3 wrong) and the non-trigger (2 wrong) to clearly document threshold behavior.

lesson: StudentConceptState objects have .mastery_score and .mastery_level making distribution computation straightforward. Score histogram with 5 equal-width buckets maps directly to mastery level boundaries.
anti_pattern: Do not compute mastery distribution from raw DB queries when in-memory StudentConceptState objects already have all needed fields.
effective_pattern: Wire new analytics into existing concept-report endpoint using established pattern from R13 hint_summary enrichment.

lesson: Dual raw+pct pattern from hint_summary R13 is gold standard for teacher-facing data. _RISK_LEVEL_ZH mapping centralizes translation. Changing struggling_concepts from List[str] to List[dict] with concept_id+display_name is backward-incompatible but necessary for teacher readability.
anti_pattern: Do not leave raw 0-1 decimals without _pct companions in teacher-facing output.
effective_pattern: Use a centralized _pct_str helper and _ZH mapping dicts for consistent formatting across all report sections.

lesson: Place summary function LAST in enrichment chain so it can consume mastery_distribution, hint_summary, and base report. Testing with fully assembled report_dict ensures realistic integration.
anti_pattern: Do not generate summary from raw data - always consume the enriched report dict to avoid duplication.
effective_pattern: Build summary by extracting key fields from already-enriched report dict, using get() with defaults for optional sections.

lesson: Severity thresholds (70%/40% not-mastered) map well to teacher intuition about class health. Wrapping enrich_blocking_concepts inside report_to_dict keeps enrichment transparent to callers. Simple count comparisons are effective and readable for pattern detection.
anti_pattern: Do not present blocking_score as a raw number - teachers cannot interpret composite scores without severity classification.
effective_pattern: Add severity_zh + pattern_zh + recommended_actions_zh for complete decision support on each blocking concept.

lesson: Replacing random.choice with min/max + key function is a minimal, high-impact change. Passing concept_states dict to strategy functions enables score-aware + staleness-aware prioritization without adding new data dependencies.
anti_pattern: Random concept selection wastes practice time on nearly-mastered concepts while ignoring those close to promotion.
effective_pattern: Closest-to-promotion for developing (push over threshold), stalest-first for review (prevent further decay), highest-score for approaching (accelerate to mastered).

lesson: Using get_all_prerequisites for transitive chain + sorting by weakest mastery_score targets the root gap. Prerequisite regression detection as highest priority prevents building on crumbling foundations.
anti_pattern: Only checking direct prerequisites misses deep dependency chain gaps that cause repeated remediation.
effective_pattern: detect_prerequisite_regression as priority-0 strategy + transitive weakest-first remediation fallback.

lesson: Trend from mastery_score vs recent_accuracy with 0.10 threshold yields clean 3-state classification. Prerequisite gap warning leverages existing get_prerequisites. Both features are additive fields that do not change existing behavior.
anti_pattern: Generic tips without trend data leave parents guessing whether child is improving or stagnating.
effective_pattern: trend + trend_zh + prereq_gap_warning as additive fields on ConceptProgress, computed from existing StudentConceptState data.

lesson: Domain-based zones mapped from concept_taxonomy give natural grouping. Weighted progress formula (mastered=100,approaching=70,developing=30) is more informative than binary complete/incomplete.
anti_pattern: Per-concept zone_unlocked boolean gives no aggregate view of domain progress.
effective_pattern: ZoneProgress dataclass per domain with weighted progress_pct and is_complete flag provides clear learning path visualization.

lesson: Prerequisite depth is a natural difficulty proxy: 0 prereqs=easy, 1-3=normal, 4+=hard. Challenge pool is always concept + get_all_prerequisites for comprehensive coverage.
anti_pattern: Boolean boss_unlocked flag with no challenge content is meaningless. Boss needs concrete challenge pool.
effective_pattern: BossChallenge with challenge_concept_ids from transitive prereqs + difficulty from depth scaling.

lesson: Badge delta detection is a simple set difference on badge_type. Dead code (streak/no-hint/comeback badges) needs explicit wiring in service.py to pass required inputs. recovered_concepts requires comparing mastery level before and after update_mastery.
anti_pattern: Computing badges without passing all required inputs leaves badge types as dead code. No delta detection means frontend cannot highlight newly earned badges.
effective_pattern: detect_new_badges(current, prev_types) for delta. Track old_level before update_mastery to detect REVIEW_NEEDED to MASTERED transitions for comeback badge.

lesson: gamification.py was listed in .gitignore causing all commits R23-R25 to silently skip it. git add -A respects .gitignore. Always run git status --short after git add to verify expected files appear. File-level grep on .gitignore for any new module name before committing.
anti_pattern: Trusting git add -A without verifying the staging area. Committing with descriptive messages about files that were never actually staged.
effective_pattern: After git add, run git diff --cached --name-only to confirm all intended files are staged. Check .gitignore for any pattern that might match new files before first commit.

lesson: When inserting new Pydantic BaseModel classes between existing model definitions, verify that fields from adjacent models are not accidentally displaced. The topic_key and seed fields from PracticeNextRequest got moved into RemediationPlanRequest causing AttributeError at runtime.
anti_pattern: Inserting model class definitions without checking field membership of neighboring classes.
effective_pattern: After any model edit, run the existing endpoint tests for adjacent models to catch field displacement early.

lesson: recordAttempt has deep DB calls through get_concept_state -> _row_to_state -> MasteryLevel(row[mastery_level]) which fails on MagicMock objects. Use tmp_db fixture with real SQLite and ensure test events include all required fields especially timestamp.
anti_pattern: Using MagicMock for connection object when the function under test makes deep nested DB calls.
effective_pattern: Use tempfile.mkstemp + real SQLite schema initialization (tmp_db fixture) for any test touching recordAttempt.

lesson: HintSession dataclass can be reconstructed from DB state (la_attempt_events.hints_viewed_count and wrong count per concept) without needing a dedicated la_hint_sessions persistence table. This avoids schema changes while still enabling adaptive escalation.
anti_pattern: Creating new DB tables when existing data can reconstruct the needed state.
effective_pattern: Query existing tables to reconstruct state objects for stateless-per-request adaptive logic.

lesson: When recordAttempt returns enriched data (remediation_concepts, mastery), the API response builder must explicitly include those fields. Default pattern: use .get(key, []) with isinstance guard to handle both dict and non-dict learning_ack values.
anti_pattern: Discarding computed fields from internal service responses in the API layer.
effective_pattern: Always audit what the service returns vs what the endpoint exposes. Use .get() with safe defaults for optional enrichment fields.

lesson: When activating dormant mastery deltas, move AnswerEvent construction inside the per-concept loop so each concept gets its own flags. Use concept domain metadata (application) and state (REVIEW_NEEDED) for heuristic detection. Always verify skill_tags actually resolve to the expected concept_id in tests - use TOPIC_TAG_TO_CONCEPT mapping.
anti_pattern: Building one AnswerEvent outside the loop and reusing it for all concepts when per-concept flags differ.
effective_pattern: Build AnswerEvent per-concept inside the mastery loop. Detect transfer via domain metadata, detect review via state level.

lesson: When unifying reports from old RBAC tables to learning module tables, avoid RBAC dependency by taking student_ids directly. The old RBAC tables use INTEGER student IDs while learning module uses TEXT - v2 works purely with TEXT. Preserve v1 for backward compatibility.
anti_pattern: Trying to join RBAC tables with learning tables when they use different ID types and schemas.
effective_pattern: Create a parallel v2 function that takes student_ids directly and queries only la_* tables. Let caller handle student ID resolution.

lesson: When expanding content guides, add English domain key aliases alongside Chinese skill_tag entries so lookups work regardless of whether the caller uses taxonomy domain names or Chinese labels. Use frozen dataclass instances shared via alias references (no duplication).
anti_pattern: Duplicating TeachingGuide content for each alias key.
effective_pattern: Define guides under Chinese keys, then add alias entries pointing to the same objects.

lesson: When backlog says empty test stubs, verify first - they may already have content. Use grep for import statements to find truly uncovered modules. Five modules had 0-1 test imports: mastery_config, validator, remediation, datasets, parent_report. A single test file covering all gaps is efficient for additive-only changes.
anti_pattern: Assuming backlog hypotheses are still valid without verifying current state.
effective_pattern: Grep for from learning.MODULE import across all test files to find coverage gaps, then create one consolidated test file.

lesson: bcrypt dual-verify pattern: try bcrypt.checkpw first, if stored hash lacks $2b$ prefix fall back to legacy hash with hmac.compare_digest, then lazy re-hash on success. Keeps backward compat with zero downtime migration.
anti_pattern: Direct == comparison for tokens/passwords enables timing attacks.
effective_pattern: hmac.compare_digest for all secret comparisons; bcrypt with built-in salt for password storage.

lesson: Debug endpoints were nested inside parent_weekly function after a return - completely dead code. Always check indentation when surveying endpoints. Moving to top level and adding env var guard fixes both the dead code and security issues.
anti_pattern: Nesting route definitions inside other route functions.
effective_pattern: Top-level route definitions with _is_dev_mode() guard returning 404 (not 403) to prevent route enumeration.

lesson: int(os.environ.get(NAME,DEFAULT)) is clean pattern for numeric config. CORS_ORIGINS as comma-separated string with split+strip handles multi-origin and single-wildcard cases.
anti_pattern: Hardcoding security-relevant constants without env var override.
effective_pattern: Always provide safe defaults that match current behavior to avoid breaking changes on upgrade.

lesson: Service functions with complex parameters should use POST with Pydantic body rather than GET with query params. Enables validation and defaults.
anti_pattern: Leaving service functions unwired when they have full DB logic built in.
effective_pattern: Import service function, add Pydantic model, wire with auth+ownership checks, test route registration + service integration.

lesson: When extracting endpoints from a monolithic server.py to APIRouter sub-modules, use lazy imports (import server as _srv inside each handler function body) to avoid circular import issues. The router module imports nothing from server at module level.
anti_pattern: Importing server helpers at module level in router files causes circular imports since server.py includes the router.
effective_pattern: APIRouter + lazy import + include_router at inline position in server.py (after all helpers are defined).

lesson: For endpoints spanning multiple URL prefixes (e.g. /v1/learning/, /v1/student/, /v1/practice/, /v1/adaptive/), use APIRouter without prefix and specify full paths in each decorator. This avoids prefix-mismatch issues and keeps route definitions explicit.
anti_pattern: Trying to use multiple APIRouters for closely related endpoints with different prefixes - adds complexity without benefit.
effective_pattern: Single APIRouter(tags=[group_name]) + full path in each @router.get/post decorator.

lesson: FastAPI registers separate APIRoute objects for GET and POST on the same path. Route introspection helper must merge methods per path using set union to avoid dictionary overwrites. Parametrized route existence tests scale well for large API surfaces.
anti_pattern: Using dict assignment for route introspection when multiple methods share a path.
effective_pattern: _routes() helper merges methods via set union; parametrized test lists make adding new endpoint contracts a one-liner.

lesson: When inserting new accumulator logic into an existing for-loop, be careful not to duplicate existing increment operations. The correct_with_hint counter ended up with two increment sites after R42 code insertion, causing 2x inflation.
anti_pattern: Copy-pasting accumulator increments without removing the original.
effective_pattern: Single increment site per counter, evidence chain fields parsed from extra_json with null-safe defaults.

lesson: Hint outcome metrics alone are not enough for teacher-facing decisions. When telemetry fields are optional or frontend-sourced, analytics should publish explicit coverage rates so low telemetry completeness is not misread as low pedagogical effectiveness.
anti_pattern: Reporting hint_success_rate without any signal about whether hint_sequence or hint_open_ts actually exist in the underlying data.
effective_pattern: Pair every advanced hint-effectiveness metric with coverage indicators and surface those indicators in the same teacher summary block.

lesson: When many pages already share `setCurrentQuestion()` and `appendAttempt()`, the lowest-risk way to add new hint telemetry is to record trace state in the shared hint engine and attach it in shared attempt telemetry as fallback. This avoids dozens of page-local edits while preserving any page that already emits explicit trace fields.
anti_pattern: Editing each question page separately to add hint_sequence/hint_open_ts even though the repo already has shared hint and attempt bottlenecks.
effective_pattern: Reset trace on `setCurrentQuestion()`, record each hint open from shared button hooks, and let `appendAttempt()` fill missing hint_sequence/hint_open_ts/hint_level_used automatically.

lesson: E2E pipeline tests that send a frontend-shaped attempt event through recordAttempt() and check get_hint_effectiveness_stats() catch integration bugs that unit tests at each layer miss. Page coverage auditing reveals that custom/legacy pages (coach, mixed-multiply) are the main gap, not missing shared wiring. Permanent audit scripts enable CI-level detection when new pages are added without shared hint engine adoption.
anti_pattern: Testing only analytics.py in isolation without verifying that evidence fields survive the validator→service→DB→analytics roundtrip.
effective_pattern: Build E2E tests that exercise the full recordAttempt→analytics pipeline with realistic payloads. Create permanent audit scripts that classify every page so coverage is tracked automatically.

lesson: normalizeAttemptForCloud() and _sanitize_practice_event() are allowlist-based — any new field added to the evidence chain MUST be explicitly listed in both functions. Two separate normalizeAttemptForCloud() implementations exist (student_auth.js and report_data_builder.js); both must be updated together. recordPracticeResult() event construction is a third choke point. Always audit all three when adding new sync fields.

Shared hint engine auto-hooking (MutationObserver + capture-phase click on #btnHint) makes legacy page migration a 3-line change: (1) add script tag, (2) call init({moduleId}), (3) call setCurrentQuestion(q) per question. Existing custom hint display is unaffected.

Mock data fixtures matching backend output shapes (format_hint_summary_for_teacher) serve as both development enabler and API contract documentation. When the live API is wired, only the data source changes.

Node.js is installed at C:/Program Files/nodejs/node.exe but not in PATH for this terminal. Use full path or add to PATH. Node v24 test runner TAP reporter gives reliable pass/fail counts. exam-sprint-gate.spec.mjs requires @playwright/test which is not installed.

issue: hint_level_used buried in extra_json prevented indexing, efficient filtering, and required JSON parsing in every analytics query
root_cause: R42-R43 stored hint evidence fields as pass-through in extra_json rather than as first-class columns, because the schema was already established
effective_fix: Add migration 004 with ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for hint_level_used and success_after_hint. Update validator to extract+validate these fields from both top-level and extra dict. Update service INSERT to store them. Update analytics to prefer column with extra_json fallback.
risk_note: Historical rows have NULL in new columns. Analytics fallback to extra_json handles this gracefully.
verification_rule: After promoting any field from extra_json to first-class column, always: (1) add migration, (2) update validator extraction, (3) update service INSERT, (4) update analytics queries with fallback, (5) test with both new-format and legacy-format data
next_time_instruction: When a field in extra_json is queried in >2 analytics functions, promote it to a first-class column with a migration.

issue: Cross-device parent-report flows still failed silently when the browser had no backend base configured, even though the sync registry and tests already existed.
root_cause: The sync contract depended on AIMATH_PARENT_REPORT_API_BASE, AIMATH_API_BASE, or ?api=, but the frontend had no fallback discovery path and did not clearly tell the user whether the current device was cloud-connected or local-only.
effective_fix: Keep the existing backend-owned registry flow, but add low-risk frontend resilience: same-origin fallback for non-GitHub hosts, runtime backend-config.json probing, retry-on-detect for sync/fetch paths, backend-ready event dispatch, and explicit cloud-status UI on both student and parent surfaces.
failed_approach: Treating this as a backend schema problem or rewriting the working sync pipeline. The actual product gap was configuration discovery and user-visible state, not the registry endpoint design.
risk_note: If production uses GitHub Pages plus a separate backend, a real backend URL still must be published in backend-config.json or injected as a global. Auto-detection cannot guess a host that the repo never declares.
verification_rule: For cross-device report readiness, verify both transport and operator visibility: backend base can be resolved or reported missing, student sync retries after detection, parent login page shows backend availability, registry round-trip tests still pass, and source-level security tests still enforce backend-owned writes.
next_time_instruction: Before adding new parent-report UX, check whether the device is truly cloud-connected and surface that state first; do not let local-only mode masquerade as cross-device sync.
